---
pagetitle: "Workshop 2026 - ML-based workflow to enhance seismic fault interpretation in OBN seismic data"
toc: true
toc-location: right
page-layout: article
---

::: {.page-header}
[Invited Talk]{.page-header-badge .badge-upcoming}

# ML-based workflow to enhance seismic fault interpretation in OBN seismic data

[Matheus Nilo · Invited Speaker]{.tagline}

::: {.meta}
[Day 2]{} [June 2, 2026]{} [AIEPS Workshop 2026]{} [15 min]{}
:::

::: {.actions}
[Back to Programme](../2026.qmd#programme){.btn-act .secondary}
[Speaker Profile](https://www.linkedin.com/in/matheus-nilo-68b8a1145/){.btn-act .primary target="_blank"}
:::
:::

::: {.content-body .session-detail}

## Abstract

In this work, I propose deep learning approaches that can be successfully applied to the challenge of fault interpretation in pre-salt seismic data. I first review some conventional seismic interpretation methods and deep learning-based methods. Next, I propose a workflow for synthetic data generation. Following a description of convolutional neural networks and how these models are trained, I analyze and evaluate model performance. Finally, I present application in field examples and discuss their merits from a qualitative perspective.

## Speaker Bio

[Matheus Nilo](https://www.linkedin.com/in/matheus-nilo-68b8a1145/) is an invited speaker at AIEPS 2026 and will present in hybrid format.

## Materials and Images

- Slides: To be added.
- Related links: To be added.
- Images: To be added.

:::
